passage.title: Animal Corner Board
passage.text: Our group keeps a feeding turn board next to the fish tank. After lunch, Hana checks it before story time. The feeding turn board has name tags for feeding and tank check turns for the day's job. Mr. Lee asks us to take care of the class pet on time. Sometimes we compare today's tag with yesterday's tag and notice one small change. We like the feeding turn board because it helps us finish pet work safely.

questions.prompt: What is this passage mostly about?
questions.rationale: The whole passage explains how the feeding turn board is used.

questions.prompt: Where is the feeding turn board?
questions.rationale: The first sentence says the feeding turn board is next to the fish tank.

questions.prompt: When does Hana use the feeding turn board?
questions.rationale: The second sentence says Hana uses it after lunch.

questions.prompt: What does the feeding turn board have?
questions.rationale: The third sentence says the feeding turn board has name tags.

questions.prompt: What does compare mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Compare means to look at two things and think about them together.

questions.prompt: What does notice mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Notice means to see and pay attention to something.
